pages.title: Why Every SMB Needs a Second Brain
pages.body:
# Why Every SMB Needs a Second Brain

## The Problem

Small and medium businesses run on institutional knowledge — the stuff in people's heads, buried in email threads, scattered across chat tools. When someone leaves, the knowledge leaves with them. When you need to recall a client detail from 6 months ago, you're searching 3 different apps.

## The Solution

A second brain that:

- **Ingests from email** — zero behavior change required
- **Connects everything** — people, companies, meetings, decisions, all linked
- **Surfaces patterns** — the knowledge graph shows connections you'd never see in a folder
- **Survives turnover** — when someone leaves, their knowledge stays

## The gbrain Advantage

Most knowledge management tools fail because they ask people to change how they work. gbrain doesn't — it plugs into the one tool everyone already uses: email.

Forward a meeting note. BCC the brain on a client email. Send yourself a quick thought. The pipeline handles the rest.

## Real Results

Companies using gbrain report:

- **60% less time** searching for information
- **Zero onboarding** for the email pipeline — it's already in their workflow
- **Complete context** before every client call — open the dashboard, search the name, everything's there

## Getting Started

1. Set up your workspace (15 minutes)
2. Configure your email address (5 minutes)
3. Start forwarding — the brain builds itself

pages.title: StartupXYZ
pages.body:
# StartupXYZ

**Industry:** Fintech (B2B payments infrastructure)
**HQ:** Singapore
**Size:** 35 employees, Series A
**Founded:** 2023

**Key people:** [[Alex Chen]] (CTO)

**Why they matter:** Early-stage fintech exploring AI adoption. Perfect candidate for Maloha Coast's AI consulting services — they have the engineering talent but need guidance on AI strategy and implementation.

**Opportunity:** Demo the gbrain email-to-dashboard pipeline as an internal knowledge management solution. If the CTO sees value, this opens a consulting engagement for broader AI adoption.

pages.title: The Email-to-Brain Pipeline
pages.body:
# The Email-to-Brain Pipeline

## What It Is

A fully automated system that turns emails into a searchable knowledge graph. Send an email → it appears in your gbrain dashboard within 2 minutes.

## How It Works

1. **Send** an email to your workspace address (e.g., `info+hermes@malohacoast.com`)
2. **Pipeline polls** Gmail every 2 minutes via the watchdog
3. **Content is extracted** — cleaned of email noise, classified by type (meeting, article, bookmark, newsletter)
4. **Entities are detected** — people, companies, and concepts mentioned in the content get their own pages
5. **Graph is wired** — wikilinks connect everything into a knowledge graph
6. **Dashboard updates** — the gbrain dashboard refreshes with the new content

## Why It Matters

Email is the universal inbox. Everyone uses it. The pipeline removes friction entirely — you don't need to open a new app, learn a new interface, or change your workflow. Just forward an email and your second brain updates itself.

## Key Features

- **2-minute turnaround** from send to dashboard
- **Entity extraction** — people and companies auto-detected
- **Tag routing** — `+hermes`, `+zaim`, `+certava` prefixes route to different dashboards
- **Dedup protection** — same email never ingested twice
- **Full-text search** — every word in every email is searchable

pages.title: Demo Meeting — gbrain Pipeline Showcase
pages.body:
# Demo Meeting — gbrain Pipeline Showcase

**Date:** 2026-07-02
**Attendees:** [[Alex Chen]] (StartupXYZ), Sayf (Maloha Coast)
**Duration:** 30 minutes

## Agenda

1. Walk through the gbrain architecture
2. Live demo: send an email → watch it appear in the dashboard
3. Discuss StartupXYZ's knowledge management needs
4. Explore integration possibilities

## Key Discussion Points

- **Alex's pain point:** Engineering team documentation scattered across Slack, Notion, and email. No single source of truth.
- **Sayf's demo:** Showed the email pipeline in action — sent a meeting note from phone, it appeared in the dashboard in under 2 minutes.
- **Alex's reaction:** "This is exactly what we need. The email entry point means zero adoption friction."

## Action Items

- [ ] Send Alex the gbrain setup guide
- [ ] Schedule follow-up with StartupXYZ's engineering lead
- [ ] Prepare a scoping document for pilot deployment

## Next Steps

If StartupXYZ moves forward, this would be a 2-week pilot:
- Week 1: Set up gbrain instance, configure email pipeline, train team
- Week 2: Migrate existing docs, integrate with Slack, iterate on dashboard

pages.title: Alex Chen
pages.body:
# Alex Chen

**Role:** CTO at StartupXYZ, an early-stage fintech in Singapore.

**Background:** 12 years in distributed systems, previously at Grab and Stripe. Looking to adopt AI for internal developer tools.

**Key interests:**
- AI-assisted code review
- Knowledge management for engineering teams
- gbrain for team documentation

**Last contact:** Q3 2026 — requested a demo of the gbrain pipeline.
